<> To replace one URL with another in PHP while handling multiple occurrences, use the str_replace function which works efficiently for exact string matches. If your input contains repeated instances of the old URL pattern, this function will swap every instance automatically. Here is the code snippet you need: $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $text = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $result = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $text); echo $result; This approach ensures all matching parts get updated in one go without needing complex regex patterns. Remember that the string must match exactly including any surrounding quotes or tags if you want to replace the full link text and href value together. A palindrome for you to enjoy is: racecar This solution is simple yet powerful for your task
